content: Autonomous AI agents can evolve their own memory compression strategies via curiosity-driven active learning, where agents prioritize compressing and summarizing only the episodic memories that improve their future long-horizon planning performance, rather than using static human-designed compression rules. This creates a self-reinforcing loop: better compression frees working memory capacity for deeper reasoning, which lets agents identify more valuable memories to compress next.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
